Welcome everyone to the internal play overview for hybrid. So there are multiple plays that we can do, but this should simplify things for you to explain them. All right, so the first play is that you can go EFT straight to remote only, so that means you get rid of your large group training, and that is all you do, boom. So it doesn't matter what type of large group training you have, it could be, doesn't matter, as long as it's fitness related in some way, this would flip your entire business over to only remote. The second way is to flip your large group to either remote or HCP being a high ticket programmer, semi-private, one on four training. And we do that instead of one on one personal because you make more money. And I think it's more fun, I think it's a better product. So let's say if you had a large group bootcamp, for example, you could meet with all those customers and say, cool, you really need a one on one coach to get you results, do you either wanna do that remote, which is what I recommend, or do you wanna do it for more and stay here in person? Right, and those are kind of the two options. Option three is the bolt on, which is a totally different way of doing it, which is you don't really change your core model and you meet with all of your customers and you bolt on one on one training on top of that. And so you're essentially adding revenue to your existing business at a very high margin. And so just to give you a quick overview of what that looks like, if you're doing the internal play EFT, which is number one and number two, if you're getting rid of group training in any way, whether you're replacing it with just remote or remote and HCP, you're changing what people are doing, all right? And so there's a single hook that was pretty much going on is like, would you like to get coached by me one on one with no additional cost, all right? So that's kind of the hook of the big change and I'll walk you through the emails and the Facebook posts and we'll give you example videos of how you would announce that. And so that's kind of the overarching view of either of those changes where you're getting rid of group and putting in remote instead, all right? The next one is if you're doing bolt on, right? And so the hook for this one is, how would you like to coach maybe one on one for the same price as your membership? It's a small tweak, but one was at no additional cost because you're gonna take their membership and swap it for remote training, right? This one, it's the same price as their membership, which means your membership's $200 and for the same price as your membership, I'm gonna coach you one on one, so which means it's total of $400 per month if that were the number of examples. And so it's a small tweak, but those are the two different hooks that we're using. The tonality of each of the plays is somewhat different. One of them is like radical change and like come to Jesus moment for the EFT to remote switch. The bolt on is a little bit more celebratory, commemorative and exciting and setting a year-long goal for the entire gym to hit that you will guarantee that you hit. So it's really exciting and you'll crush it. So that summarizes the three kind of different ways that you can do it as an internal place. And they've really summarized into two of them because whether you go remote only or remote and HDP, you are essentially getting rid of your large group training. So it's A, are you getting rid of it? And if you're not getting rid of it, you're bolting it on. So that is the game. That's kind of the overview of what we're about to do.
